stories.headline: Ukraine hits Moscow refinery as Zelenskyy seeks Trump support to end war
stories.summary: Russia's Defence Ministry says air defences shot down 555 Ukrainian drones over several regions overnight.

stories.headline: Moscow oil refinery struck in Ukraine’s biggest air raid on city since start of war

stories.headline: ‘If Ukraine burns, your Moscow will burn,’ Zelenskyy warns after overnight strikes in Russia – Europe live
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president ramps up rhetoric after overnight drone strikes on Russian capitalHegseth makes it clear that the review will not be just a box-ticking exercise.“It’s a review that some countries will fail and others will pass with flying colours. stories.headline: US and Iran sign deal as Trump vows to release frozen funds and ease sanctions
stories.summary: President says Iranians will receive incentives when they ‘behave’ and acknowledges Tehran will keep ballistic missiles

stories.headline: US Signs Deal With Iran, But Some Trump Allies Not Happy
stories.summary: President Donald Trump signed an interim deal to end the war with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but some Republicans say the US is giving up too much. Laura Davison reports on how the deal came together and what comes next.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: U.S. and Iran have signed deal to end war, but tough negotiations remain
stories.summary: The president defended the agreement, but it has been criticized as a return to the prewar status quo.

stories.headline: ‘If Ukraine burns, so will Moscow,’ Zelenskyy says after refinery fire
stories.summary: The Ukrainian president said on Thursday that Kyiv will respond to all Russian attacks and that the morning strike on Moscow’s refinery — in response to Russia's attack on a UNESCO-protected cathedral earlier this week was “entirely justified.”

stories.headline: Oil prices fall, stocks rally as US, Iran sign framework to end war
stories.summary: Brent crude drops 2.3 percent, while key stock indices in Japan, South Korea and Taiwan climb.

stories.headline: Hegseth announces US review of Europe forces, says some allies will fail
stories.summary: The US defence secretary's move follows a US decision to scale back its commitments to a high readiness force within the alliance.

stories.headline: Hegseth unveils six-month review of US military presence in Europe
stories.summary: Defence secretary announced decision as he scolded Nato allies for failing to support Washington in Iran war

stories.headline: US and Iran sign deal, but who really won? Here's what to know
stories.summary: Trump signed the framework deal at Versailles while Pezeshkian signed in Tehran, pledging to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and triggering 60 days of further talks. Analysts say the terms leave Tehran in a stronger position than the deal's framing suggests.

stories.headline: Moscow hit by largest Ukrainian attack since start of Russia's full-scale war
stories.summary: A refinery and a shopping centre were burning after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

stories.headline: Pentagon chief announces review of US forces in Europe, slams NATO allies
stories.summary: Pete Hegseth says the review would last for up to six months and include consultations with Congress.

stories.headline: World reacts to US-Iran deal to extend ceasefire, reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Several countries welcome deal, as IAEA chief says 'now the technical work starts' on Iran's nuclear programme.

stories.headline: Israel says one soldier killed and several injured in clashes following latest strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli officials close to Netanyahu say the Likud leader has no intention to give up positions in Lebanon and are engaged in negotiations with the US following the announcement of an initial deal with Iran to halt hostilities.

stories.headline: Besides Trump, nobody believes deal will last, former US general says
stories.summary: Retired US General Ben Hodges believes the situation is now "much worse" than four months ago. With Israel and Lebanon left out of the deal between the US and Iran, Hodges thinks the potential for Israel and Hezbollah to "resume fighting is really high".

stories.headline: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
stories.summary: The 14-paragraph memo includes an end to fighting, an agreement that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon, and a $300bn redevelopment package for Iran.

stories.headline: Bolivia signs $20m deal with US to fight drug trafficking
stories.summary: Under its new president, Bolivia is re-establishing severed ties with the US to combat organised crime.

stories.headline: China’s tribute system and the new world order
stories.summary: Exercising power without resorting to war could be Beijing’s ultimate show of strength

stories.headline: Goldman Sees More Two-Year Volatility Under Warsh Fed
stories.summary: Kay Haigh at Goldman Sachs Asset Management says two-year Treasury yields may become more volatile as Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh charts a new course for central bank communications. He speaks with Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: BOE's Bailey Says Holding Rates Is a 'Sensible Decision'
stories.summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey tells broadcasters energy prices remain above pre-war levels and that the the central bank needs to assess "how much damage has been done to the infrastructure for supplying energy" in the Middle East. "I think holding is the right position to be in at the moment." Bailey speaks in an interview following the BOE's decision to keep interest rates at 3.75% in a 7-2 vote.  (Source: Bloomberg)

As will a plethora of Reform advisers, backroom staff and figures such as Ben Delo, a British crypto billionaire who has given £4m to Nigel Farage’s party.Yet as populist-right politicians from across the globe and their multimillionaire backers pre

stories.headline: Irish parliament votes to remove three-day abortion wait
stories.summary: Waiting period before receiving medication was included in draft law to gain support for abortion ban in 2018 referendumIreland’s parliament has voted to remove a mandatory three-day wait for abortion during early pregnancy after campaigners said the rule was an unnecessary restriction.The Dáil passed the bill on Wednesday night, clearing a path for the legislation to go to a parliamentary committee and become law later this, or next, year. Conti

stories.headline: EU-backed green bonds risk financing Chinese clean tech in third countries
stories.summary: The EU has launched a €15-20bn green investment fund to boost sustainable projects, but a good share of the money is set to go towards buying Chinese clean technologies – including the same high-risk power inverters Brussels is trying to phase out.

stories.headline: JPMorgan Chase cuts off Anthropic access for its Hong Kong staff
stories.summary: Bank follows Goldman Sachs in preventing use of Claude in Asian financial hub

stories.headline: Argentina MSCI Review Holds Key to Foreign Investment Surge
stories.summary: MSCI Inc. will decide next week whether to set Argentina on the road back to membership in global stock indexes, a move that could trigger a rush of funds into the illiquid local market.

stories.headline: Intel Shares Soar After Trump Says It Struck Apple Chip Deal
stories.summary: Intel Corp.’s stock surged Thursday after US President Donald Trump said the chipmaker will work alongside Apple Inc. to design and produce semiconductors domestically.

stories.headline: The Crypto-Treasury Dream Unravels After a 90% Stock Plunge
stories.summary: The business model of launching a public company to buy crypto is falling apart. As a result, those in the queue to do so through blank-check companies are facing pressure from investors against a market backdrop that is fiercely unfriendly.

stories.headline: The Squeeze on Liquidity Is Just Getting Started
stories.summary: The stock market’s supports are falling away as liquidity tightens and rates become increasingly restrictive.

stories.headline: SpaceX Demand Is 'Off the Charts,' Says Schwab CEO
stories.summary: Charles Schwab had one of the five busiest trading days ever when SpaceX made its stock-market debut last week. “If you think about all the market events we’ve had over those 50 years, for the IPO to crack the top five is pretty unbelievable,” Schwab Chief Executive Officer Rick Wurster said Thursday on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Anthropic Ban Forces Investor Rethink of Political Risk
stories.summary: Investors in the ever-hotter AI stock rally must suddenly consider a risk with the potential to be even more damaging than high valuations and big spending: Politics getting in the way.

stories.headline: Hormuz Is Open But Shipowners Still Have Questions About Safety
stories.summary: Oil flows through the waterway are surging, but some doubts remain.

stories.headline: Indonesia Taps Insider to Revive World’s Worst Stock Market
stories.summary: Indonesia named capital markets veteran Jeffrey Hendrik as chief executive of its stock exchange, the latest effort to bolster investor confidence in the world’s worst-performing equity market.

stories.headline: Barack Obama Presidential Center Opens in Chicago
stories.summary: Today’s opening is expected to draw four former presidents, as well as Bruce Springsteen, Bono and Jennifer Hudson. Chicago has seen years of planning and legal fights.

stories.headline: Billionaire Tax Officially Has Enough Signatures for California Ballot
stories.summary: A proposed wealth tax on billionaires is headed to the ballot unless Gov. Gavin Newsom and other opponents reach a deal with backers to remove it.

stories.headline: How the stock market became Trump’s most favored adviser
stories.summary: Trump has increasingly treated the stock market as a real-time referendum on his presidency, citing market gains as justification for many consequential decisions.

stories.headline: Real Madrid sign Ibrahima Konate to multi-year deal from Liverpool
stories.summary: Acquisition of the French defender, who is currently at the World Cup, is a key signing by new Real coach Jose Mourinho.

stories.headline: Taiwan hopes US arms sale package can be approved soon, president says
stories.summary: Taiwan’s President William Lai Ching-te reiterates his desire for talks based on 'parity and respect' with China.

stories.headline: 'It was surreal': British couple describe having warning shots fired near them by Russian warship
stories.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

stories.headline: Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC
stories.summary: Japan needs to revisit the pacifist posture that has defined it since World War Two, Shinjiro Koizumi says.

stories.summary: The proposal is to remove the three-day wait for an abortion in early pregnancy in the Republic of Ireland.

stories.headline: Iran sends tankers loaded with oil past US military blockade
stories.summary: Three Iranian tankers loaded with crude oil have passed the US blockade line in the Gulf of Oman, ship-tracking data shows.

stories.headline: Extraordinary Messi makes more history in masterclass for the ages
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland both enjoyed a fine start to the World Cup - but on a day packed with superstars, Lionel Messi still took top billing.

stories.summary: Every day we will deliver concise analysis of the stories driving business and markets worldwide

stories.headline: Federal Prisons Must Provide Hormones Sought by Trans Inmates
stories.summary: The government had argued that a “pendulum swing” in the medical consensus justified treating trans inmates with psychotherapy and antidepressants instead.

stories.headline: Bill Pulte, a Bulldog for Trump, Prepares to Take Reins at Spy Agency
stories.summary: Democrats and some Republicans excoriated President Trump’s pick for a top national security job. The president pushed ahead anyway.

stories.headline: Trump Rescinds Endorsement of Right-Wing Pastor After Texting Scandal
stories.summary: The pastor, Jackson Lahmeyer, dropped out of the race for a House seat in Oklahoma as President Trump backed Mr. Lahmeyer’s Republican rival in a runoff election.

stories.headline: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
stories.summary: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese cultureThe activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar.The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a

stories.headline: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
stories.summary: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

stories.headline: Trump may release US-Iran deal before Friday, Vance says
stories.summary: The US vice-president says the agreement is "about a page and a half" and "very general", meaning many details will be worked out later.

stories.headline: Fujitsu chair resigns after ‘woman-related inappropriate conduct’
stories.summary: Japanese technology company at centre of Post Office IT scandal is negotiating settlement with UK government over faulty softwareBusiness live – latest updatesThe chair of Fujitsu, the Japanese technology firm at the centre of the Post Office IT scandal, has resigned after its board became aware of his “woman-related inappropriate conduct”.The company said on Tuesday that Hidenori Furuta had stepped down after two years in the role. stories.headline: Venezuela signs deal with US energy giant to rebuild power grid
stories.summary: The deal with General Electric is the latest sign of co-operation between US firms and Venezuela's interim government.

stories.headline: Hungary's MPs block return of Orbán, limiting rule of PM to eight years
stories.summary: The vote fulfils a promise by new PM Péter Magyar to stop his predecessor from becoming prime minister again.

stories.headline: Japan raises interest rate to highest for 31 years
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

stories.headline: Trump aims to end Iran war but nuclear issue remains unresolved
stories.summary: President Trump says the US and Iran have agreed a deal to end the war but it is unclear what has been agreed on Iran's nuclear programme

stories.headline: Bowen: Iran deal ends Trump's war that revealed limit of US dominance
stories.summary: The deal to end fighting and re-open the Strait of Hormuz leaves the sides where they were 24 hours before the war - only with thousands now dead.

stories.headline: Trump heralds Iran deal but questions - and risks - remain
stories.summary: The announcement of an Iran deal was a welcome birthday gift for the president – but its success or failure may hinge on the details.

stories.headline: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
stories.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

stories.headline: Dublin-born Cape Verde star recruited on LinkedIn gets World Cup chance
stories.summary: They are one of the smallest countries to play in a World Cup, but Cape Verde are aiming high, with a Dublin-born defender who used to work in a bank among their ranks.

stories.headline: South Korea's Starbucks to shut for staff history lesson after backlash
stories.summary: The chain was criticised for a "Tank Day" marketing ploy on the anniversary of a military massacre.

stories.headline: Vincent's parents 'never say he's good enough' - so he turned to a middle-aged couple online
stories.summary: A niche group of content creators called "virtual parents" have become hugely popular among young people.

stories.headline: Watch: Fans celebrate Mexico's win in World Cup opening match
stories.summary: There were wild celebrations in host nation Mexico, as their team kicked off their World Cup campaign with a 2-0 win over South Africa.
